description: Create a content slide titled 'We recommend' with a two-column layout of recommendation text blocks and footer source/page.

items.text: Brands should seek out non-digital ways to authentically connect with customers in the moments when they’re looking for textural, face-to-face experiences.
items.sub: This could be a key differentiator at a time when many brands have shifted the focus to digital. It may mean bringing additional joy to moments of fun, or being there physically when customers need a helping hand.

items.text: Organizations need to understand what people value in simpler technology.
items.sub: As people reach for simpler technologies in the moments that matter to them, the mindset they’re in when they do so is important, as is the possible effect on their expectations of a brand. The aesthetics that simple tech advocates have adopted (including film grain and digital noise) are key as they signal a brand’s relevance and understanding of the emotional desire to disconnect.

items.text: There’s an opportunity to go local—without the clichés.
items.sub: Brands can build affinity by sensitively connecting with local cultures—without stooping to stereotypes—and highlighting the texture and nuance of specific cities and regions in events, content, advertising and experiences.

Certain brands may benefit from embracing the nature-led aesthetic that is becoming increasingly important to people, and/or exploring events or even business models centered on people’s desire to get outdoors.
